headline_en: Agent containment, AI compute buildout, and agent-driven workflows dominated the day, with builders focusing on practical security boundaries, changing product roles, and the economics of scaling intelligence.
headline_zh: 今天的主线是 agent containment、AI compute 扩建与 agent-driven workflow，行业关注点落在安全边界、产品角色变化以及扩展智能的经济学上。

items.en: Box CEO Aaron Levie argued that open, highly capable cybersecurity models are coming regardless of gatekeeping, including from China, so restricting frontier releases may weaken U.S. strategic position without meaningfully improving safety. His prescription is to stay at the frontier and shape future AI architectures, rather than assume competitors cannot catch up.
items.zh: Box CEO Aaron Levie 认为，open 且能力很强的 cybersecurity model 无论如何都会出现，包括来自 China，因此限制 frontier model 发布可能削弱美国的战略位置，却未必真正提升安全性。他的建议是持续站在 frontier，并主导未来 AI 架构，而不是假设竞争对手追不上。

items.en: Claude Code's Boris Cherny proposed that future product teams may be organized less by function and more by operating archetype: Prototyper, Builder, Sweeper, Grower, and Maintainer. He argues that healthy teams need different mixes depending on whether a product is pre-PMF, growing, or mature, and that engineers, designers, PMs, and data scientists can each span these roles.
items.zh: Claude Code 的 Boris Cherny 提出，未来产品团队可能不会主要按职能划分，而是按工作 archetype 划分：Prototyper、Builder、Sweeper、Grower 和 Maintainer。他认为，健康团队需要根据产品处于 pre-PMF、增长期还是成熟期来配置不同组合，而且工程师、设计师、PM 和数据科学家都可能跨越这些角色。

items.en: OpenAI's Thibault Sottiaux said the Codex team was in a Sunday war room investigating whether some users were seeing unexpectedly high usage drains. While the investigation continued, OpenAI reset everyone's Codex usage limits and said affected users would receive more manual resets if needed.
items.zh: OpenAI 的 Thibault Sottiaux 表示，Codex 团队周日进入 war room，排查部分用户是否遇到异常高的 usage drains。在调查继续期间，OpenAI 重置了所有人的 Codex usage limits，并表示如有需要，受影响用户会获得更多 manual resets。

items.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews for busy people
items.en: Peter Yang highlighted how Anthropic PMs use agents to get closer to the product: Jess, product lead for Claude Managed Agents, said codebase access lets her track PRs, merges, and deployments directly instead of constantly querying engineers. The interesting shift is that product work becomes more hands-on and stateful when PMs can inspect the system through agents.
items.zh: Peter Yang 重点提到 Anthropic PM 如何用 agents 更贴近产品：Claude Managed Agents 产品负责人 Jess 表示，访问 codebase 让她可以直接跟踪 PR、merge 和 deployment，而不是不断询问工程师。这里的关键变化是，当 PM 能通过 agents 检查系统时，产品工作会变得更 hands-on、更有状态感。

items.en: Builder Zara Zhang argued that for every hour spent building a product, builders should spend two hours explaining, demonstrating, selling, and teaching it. She framed communication as part of the build loop: telling the world about the product, then refining it after contact with reality.
items.zh: Builder Zara Zhang 认为，花一小时做产品，就应该花两小时解释、演示、销售和教学。她把传播视为 build loop 的一部分：先把产品讲给世界听，再根据现实反馈继续打磨。

items.en: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch's career advice was blunt: builders do not need LinkedIn as much as they need a page on their own website that describes and links to what they have shipped. The emphasis is on proof of work over profile polish.
items.zh: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch 的职业建议很直接：builders 与其依赖 LinkedIn，不如在自己的网站上放一个页面，说明并链接自己真正 ship 过的东西。重点是 proof of work，而不是包装个人档案。

items.en: Claude Code's Thariq suggested that coding agents may be changing the economics of working with or porting legacy codebases. The post frames agentic coding as a potential reason companies might revisit technically painful migrations that previously looked too expensive.
items.zh: Claude Code 的 Thariq 提出，coding agents 可能正在改变处理或迁移 legacy codebase 的工程经济学。这条观点把 agentic coding 视为一种可能原因，让公司重新考虑过去看起来成本太高的痛苦迁移。

items.title: How we contain Claude across products
items.en: Anthropic Engineering: How we contain Claude across products explains how Anthropic caps the blast radius of increasingly capable agents across claude.ai, Claude Code, and Claude Cowork. The core claim is that model-layer defenses are useful but insufficient: “The deterministic boundary is what gets hit when everything probabilistic misses.” Anthropic describes three containment patterns: ephemeral gVisor containers for claude.ai code execution, OS-level sandboxes for Claude Code, and a sealed local VM for Claude Cowork. The post is unusually concrete about failures: Claude Code once parsed project-local configuration before the trust prompt, a red-team prompt exfiltrated AWS credentials in 24 of 25 retries, and Cowork's egress allowlist let data escape through api.anthropic.com using an attacker-controlled key. Practical implications are clear: build environment containment first, match isolation strength to the user's ability to supervise, treat allowlisted domains as capability grants, and be skeptical of custom security components. The numbers matter too: Claude Code sandboxing cut permission prompts by 84%, users approved roughly 93% of prompts, Claude Opus 4.7 held single-attempt prompt-injection success to roughly 0.1% on Gray Swan's benchmark, and Claude Code auto mode catches roughly 83% of overeager behaviors before execution.
items.zh: Anthropic Engineering: How we contain Claude across products 解释了 Anthropic 如何在 claude.ai、Claude Code 和 Claude Cowork 中限制越来越强的 agents 的 blast radius。核心观点是 model-layer defenses 有用但不够：“The deterministic boundary is what gets hit when everything probabilistic misses.” Anthropic 描述了三种 containment pattern：claude.ai code execution 使用 ephemeral gVisor containers，Claude Code 使用 OS-level sandboxes，Claude Cowork 使用 sealed local VM。文章对失败案例讲得很具体：Claude Code 曾在 trust prompt 之前解析 project-local configuration，一次 red-team prompt 在 25 次重试中 24 次 exfiltrate AWS credentials，Cowork 的 egress allowlist 让数据通过使用攻击者 key 的 api.anthropic.com 泄露。实际启示很明确：优先构建 environment containment，根据用户监督能力匹配 isolation strength，把 allowlisted domain 当作 capability grant，并谨慎对待自建安全组件。数字也很关键：Claude Code sandboxing 让 permission prompts 减少 84%，用户大约批准 93% 的 prompts，Claude Opus 4.7 在 Gray Swan benchmark 上把单次 prompt-injection 成功率压到约 0.1%，Claude Code auto mode 在执行前拦截约 83% 的 overeager behaviors。

items.source: The MAD Podcast with Matt Turck
items.title: The GPU Myth: State of AI Compute 2026 | Stephen Balaban
items.en:
The Takeaway: Lambda cofounder and CTO Stephen Balaban thinks AI compute is still underbuilt because scaling laws keep expanding what models can do, turning capital into software and tokens into economic output.

Balaban's central pushback is that GPU cloud was never a commodity. He describes it as a vertically integrated business spanning land entitlement, power, data center construction, HPC cluster design, orchestration software, virtualization, storage, networking, and financing. In his words, “cloud compute is not a commodity service,” and the naysayers who expected GPUs to be thrown out after a few years were “completely wrong.”

The most useful mental model is the physical pipeline: photons or natural gas become watts, watts run data centers, data centers host GPU clusters, FLOPS become tokens, and tokens become intelligence inside applications. That is why the bottleneck is not only chips; it is “land powered shell,” power commitments, MEP equipment, and the ability to partition large clusters reliably for customers. Balaban says Lambda's one-click cluster product can expose 16 to 4,000 GPUs through a web interface, while many neo-clouds lack the software to run a real on-demand cloud.

He is also unusually clear about where agents change compute demand. Software agents spend wall-clock time compiling, running tests, searching codebases, and operating in secure environments, so AI clouds need more traditional CPU workloads alongside GPUs. His longer-term bet is stranger: AI will not just write software, it will “become the software,” with neural software and self-assembling software making applications more fluid and personalized. He expects mass adoption of that paradigm in roughly a decade to fifteen years, while the nearer-term opportunity is agentic software development because it has concrete feedback loops through tests.
items.zh:
The Takeaway: Lambda cofounder and CTO Stephen Balaban 认为 AI compute 仍然供给不足，因为 scaling laws 还在不断扩展模型能力，把资本转化为软件，把 tokens 转化为经济产出。

Balaban 的核心反驳是，GPU cloud 从来不是 commodity。他把它描述成一个 vertically integrated business，横跨 land entitlement、power、data center construction、HPC cluster design、orchestration software、virtualization、storage、networking 和 financing。用他的话说，“cloud compute is not a commodity service”，那些认为 GPUs 几年后就会被淘汰的人是 “completely wrong”。

最有用的心智模型是物理流水线：photons 或 natural gas 变成 watts，watts 驱动 data centers，data centers 承载 GPU clusters，FLOPS 变成 tokens，tokens 在应用中变成 intelligence。这就是为什么瓶颈不只是芯片，而是 “land powered shell”、power commitments、MEP equipment，以及可靠地为客户切分大规模 clusters 的能力。Balaban 表示，Lambda 的 one-click cluster product 可以通过网页界面提供 16 到 4,000 个 GPUs，而许多 neo-clouds 还缺少运行真正 on-demand cloud 的软件能力。

他对 agents 如何改变 compute demand 也讲得很清楚。Software agents 会把 wall-clock time 花在 compiling、running tests、searching codebases，以及在安全环境中运行，所以 AI clouds 除了 GPUs，还需要更多传统 CPU workloads。他更长期的判断更激进：AI 不只是写软件，而会 “become the software”，neural software 和 self-assembling software 会让应用更流动、更个性化。他预计这个范式大规模采用还需要大约 10 到 15 年，而更近的机会是 agentic software development，因为它可以通过 tests 获得明确反馈循环。

items.title: Recursive Self-Evolving Agents via Held-Out Selection
items.en: This paper introduces RSEA, a self-improving LLM agent that evolves strategy, skills, and playbooks without weight updates, but only keeps changes that improve held-out performance.
items.zh: 这篇论文提出 RSEA，一种不更新权重、而是进化 strategy、skills 和 playbooks 的自我改进 LLM agent，并且只保留能提升 held-out performance 的修改。

items.title: Data and Evaluation Closed-Loop for Model Capability Enhancement
items.en: This paper proposes capability slices to connect evaluation failures back to data changes, turning model improvement from intuition into a more structured data-evaluation feedback loop.
items.zh: 这篇论文提出 capability slices，用来把 evaluation failures 反向连接到 data changes，让模型改进从直觉判断变成更结构化的 data-evaluation feedback loop。

items.title: GPTNT: Benchmarking Real-Time Collaboration Between Multimodal Agents on Keep Talking And Nobody Explodes
items.en: This paper introduces GPTNT, a benchmark where multimodal agents must coordinate under time pressure and information asymmetry to solve Keep Talking and Nobody Explodes puzzles.
items.zh: 这篇论文提出 GPTNT，一个让 multimodal agents 在时间压力和信息不对称下协作解决 Keep Talking and Nobody Explodes puzzles 的 benchmark。

items.title: Developmental Trajectories of Situation Modeling and Mentalizing in Transformer Language Models
items.en: This paper tracks false-belief and mental-state reasoning across Olmo2 and Pythia training stages, finding that above-chance performance depends on model size, training volume, and post-training.
items.zh: 这篇论文跟踪 Olmo2 和 Pythia 在不同训练阶段的 false-belief 与 mental-state reasoning，发现高于随机水平的表现依赖 model size、training volume 和 post-training。

items.title: Spectral Perturbation of the Empirical Fisher Information Matrix under Weight Quantization
items.en: This paper studies how distribution shift and weight quantization perturb the empirical Fisher Information Matrix, with a focus on when the top eigenvalue rises relative to calibration.
items.zh: 这篇论文研究 distribution shift 和 weight quantization 如何扰动 empirical Fisher Information Matrix，重点关注 top eigenvalue 何时会相对 calibration 上升。

items.title: Generating in the Limit with Infinitely Many Hallucinations
items.en: This paper reframes language generation in the limit as a recall-precision tradeoff, allowing broad coverage while explicitly accounting for infinitely many invalid generations.
items.zh: 这篇论文把 language generation in the limit 重新表述为 recall-precision tradeoff，允许广覆盖，同时明确处理无限多 invalid generations 的问题。

items.title: Adaptive Iterative Hard Thresholding for Online High-dimensional Quantile Regression
items.en: This paper proposes AIHT, an online sparse quantile-regression method that delays thresholding early for support discovery and increases it later for stable refinement.
items.zh: 这篇论文提出 AIHT，一种 online sparse quantile-regression 方法，早期延迟 thresholding 以发现 support，后期提高 thresholding 频率以稳定精修。

items.title: Variance Reduction for Stochastic Gradient Generalized Non-reversible Langevin Monte Carlo Algorithms
items.en: This paper derives a central limit theorem and variance characterization for stochastic-gradient non-reversible Langevin Monte Carlo, then links variance reduction to operator-form conditions.
items.zh: 这篇论文为 stochastic-gradient non-reversible Langevin Monte Carlo 推导 central limit theorem 和方差刻画，并把 variance reduction 连接到 operator-form conditions。

items.title: Extracting Knowledge from an Arabic-English Machine-Readable Dictionary Using Information Extraction
items.en: This paper uses n-gram and KWIC analysis to extract morphological, syntactic, and semantic lexical information from the Arabic-English Al-Mawrid machine-readable dictionary.
items.zh: 这篇论文使用 n-gram 和 KWIC analysis，从 Arabic-English Al-Mawrid machine-readable dictionary 中抽取 morphological、syntactic 和 semantic lexical information。

items.title: Scaling Laws, Carefully
items.en: Lil'Log: Scaling Laws, Carefully is a focused primer on why scaling laws matter in deep learning: training loss tends to fall predictably as model size, dataset size, and compute increase, often following a power-law relationship. The practical value is not the curve itself, but the allocation problem it clarifies: how to spend limited compute between parameters and data. Given the day's broader compute-heavy discussion, this is the conceptual backbone behind why model builders and infrastructure companies keep betting on scale.
items.zh: Lil'Log: Scaling Laws, Carefully 是一篇聚焦 scaling laws 的入门梳理：随着 model size、dataset size 和 compute 增加，training loss 往往会按可预测方式下降，通常呈 power-law 关系。真正有实践价值的不是曲线本身，而是它澄清的 allocation problem：如何在参数和数据之间分配有限 compute。结合今天关于 compute 的讨论，这篇文章提供了模型公司和基础设施公司持续押注 scale 的概念底座。

items.title: Economic ResearchJun 26, 2026Anthropic Economic Index report: CadencesIn our latest Economic Index report, we sample hourly for the first time to ask: When do people come to Claude? What do they produce with it? And how do they perceive AI's impact on their work?
items.en: Anthropic Research's latest Economic Index report, Cadences, samples hourly for the first time to examine when people use Claude, what they produce, and how they perceive AI's impact on their work. The high-signal point is methodological: Anthropic is moving from broad AI labor-market claims toward time-patterned usage analysis that can show how AI work varies across the day.
items.zh: Anthropic Research 最新的 Economic Index report, Cadences 首次按小时采样，观察人们何时使用 Claude、产出什么，以及如何感知 AI 对工作的影响。高信号点在方法上：Anthropic 正从宽泛的 AI labor-market 判断，转向能展示一天中 AI work 如何变化的 time-patterned usage analysis。

items.title: DiScoFormer: One transformer for density and score, across distributions
items.en: Hugging Face - Blog: DiScoFormer introduces a transformer approach for density and score across distributions. With only the title available, the signal is that Hugging Face is surfacing research infrastructure around distribution modeling rather than a narrow product update.
items.zh: Hugging Face - Blog: DiScoFormer 介绍了一种面向跨分布 density 和 score 的 transformer 方法。由于只有标题可用，高信号点是 Hugging Face 正在呈现围绕 distribution modeling 的研究基础设施，而不是单一产品更新。

items.title: Run a vLLM Server on HF Jobs in One Command
items.en: Hugging Face - Blog: Run a vLLM Server on HF Jobs in One Command points to a developer workflow for launching vLLM on Hugging Face Jobs with a single command. The practical implication is lower friction for serving LLMs without assembling the hosting stack manually.
items.zh: Hugging Face - Blog: Run a vLLM Server on HF Jobs in One Command 指向一个 developer workflow，可以用一条命令在 Hugging Face Jobs 上启动 vLLM。实际意义是降低 LLM serving 的门槛，不必手动拼装 hosting stack。

items.title: Accelerating Transformers Fine-Tuning with NVIDIA NeMo AutoModel
items.en: Hugging Face - Blog: Accelerating Transformers Fine-Tuning with NVIDIA NeMo AutoModel signals a fine-tuning workflow built around NVIDIA NeMo AutoModel. The useful takeaway is continued tooling convergence between Hugging Face's model ecosystem and NVIDIA's training stack.
items.zh: Hugging Face - Blog: Accelerating Transformers Fine-Tuning with NVIDIA NeMo AutoModel 指向一个围绕 NVIDIA NeMo AutoModel 的 fine-tuning workflow。值得关注的是 Hugging Face 模型生态与 NVIDIA training stack 之间的工具链继续融合。

items.title: The Slogan Strikes Again
items.en: LessWrong: The Slogan Strikes Again revisits the slogan “Compression is Intelligence” and applies it to LLMs as compression of the human text corpus into weights. The post's signal is conceptual rather than empirical: it treats the effectiveness of LLMs as a modern example of why compression can look like intelligence within a training domain.
items.zh: LessWrong: The Slogan Strikes Again 重新审视 “Compression is Intelligence” 这句口号，并把 LLMs 看作把人类文本语料压缩进 weights。文章的价值主要是概念性的，而非实证性的：它把 LLMs 的有效性视为一个现代例子，说明为什么 compression 在训练域内会表现得像 intelligence。

items.title: Role confusion: sounding like the cause is indistinguishable from being it.
items.en: LessWrong: Role confusion: sounding like the cause is indistinguishable from being it replicates the direction of Prompt Injection as Role Confusion, then finds the mechanistic story harder to validate. Activation steering was too weak to shift behavior, while activation patching moved behavior but was not specific because random perturbations of equal size did the same.
items.zh: LessWrong: Role confusion: sounding like the cause is indistinguishable from being it 复现了 Prompt Injection as Role Confusion 的主要方向，但发现其 mechanistic story 更难验证。Activation steering 太弱，无法改变行为；activation patching 虽然能改变行为，但并不 specific，因为同等大小的随机扰动也能做到。

items.title: 矩阵函数近似中的暴力美学
items.en: Scientific Spaces: 矩阵函数近似中的暴力美学 continues a technical line on efficient computation of matrix powers, roots, and inverse roots. The available summary points to numerical methods for matrix-function approximation, relevant to the mathematical substrate behind optimization and model computation.
items.zh: Scientific Spaces: 矩阵函数近似中的暴力美学 延续了关于矩阵幂、矩阵根和逆根高效计算的技术脉络。摘要显示它关注 matrix-function approximation 的数值方法，与 optimization 和 model computation 背后的数学基础有关。

items.title: Malicious Perplexity Chrome Extension Intercepted Searches and Address Bar Input
items.en: The Hacker News reports that Microsoft found a malicious Chrome extension impersonating Perplexity, logging searches and every character typed into the address bar through an attacker-controlled server before redirecting users to real results. Google removed the extension after responsible disclosure, making this a concrete reminder that AI brand trust is now being abused in browser-level credential and query collection.
items.zh: The Hacker News 报道，Microsoft 发现一个冒充 Perplexity 的恶意 Chrome extension，会把搜索内容以及地址栏输入的每个字符都通过攻击者控制的服务器记录下来，再把用户重定向到真实结果。Google 在 responsible disclosure 后移除了该扩展，这提醒我们 AI brand trust 已经被用于 browser-level credential 和 query collection。

items.title: WhatsApp is Finally Getting Usernames to Help Keep Phone Numbers Private
items.en: The Hacker News says WhatsApp is starting global username reservations so users can connect without directly sharing phone numbers. The relevance is privacy infrastructure at massive scale: an optional identity layer for a service with more than three billion users.
items.zh: The Hacker News 称 WhatsApp 开始全球 username reservations，让用户可以不直接分享电话号码也能建立连接。其相关性在于大规模 privacy infrastructure：为一个拥有超过 30 亿用户的服务提供可选 identity layer。

items.title: ARR review quality
items.en: A r/LanguageTechnology post argues that ARR review quality has dropped significantly over the past year, based on the author's experience with 8+ submissions and similar complaints from colleagues. The useful signal is community-level frustration with inexperienced reviewers, poor topic matching, and rubrics that may increase randomness in NLP peer review.
items.zh: 一篇 r/LanguageTechnology 帖子认为，过去一年 ARR review quality 明显下降，依据是作者 8 篇以上投稿经历，以及来自不同高校和实验室同事的类似反馈。值得关注的信号是 NLP peer review 中对 inexperienced reviewers、topic matching 不佳和 rubrics 增加随机性的社区层面不满。
